data.body: Note: This behavior (creating follow-on epics without user approval) likely occurred after a context compaction event. The brainstorm session was long — by the time Epic B and the security epic were created during Phase 3 ticket integration, the earlier user approval interactions may have been compacted, reducing the orchestrator's sensitivity to the approval-before-creation principle. The orchestrator treated directional approval ('yes, split into A and B') as authorization for specific ticket content without re-presenting for review. Related: c2ac-a305 (misclassification bug).
